column4: patient opens partner QR/link → app identifies partner clinic context → patient completes basic setup and consents → patient finishes guided onboarding → patient lands on main screen

patient opens app → lands on partner-aware main screen → sees appointment readiness and doctor follow-up state → understands next best action → continues preparation, opens doctor plan, or asks a diabetes-related question → returns to updated main screen
patient opens checklist → sees required preparation steps → completes next step → checklist progress updates → patient returns to main screen or continues preparation
column7: key guidance layer for prep JTBD

column4: patient chooses to add analysis → selects camera / file / share-to-app → uploads document → system accepts document for preview and processing
column7: main input flow for patient data

column4: patient reviews uploaded document → checks readability → adjusts if needed → confirms document is good enough → continues to OCR / save
column7: improves data quality before doctor review

column4: system extracts values → patient reviews OCR result → corrects unclear fields if needed → confirms structured result → data becomes ready for use
patient opens history or analysis card → sees document + structured metrics → understands what is already stored and available for doctor prep
column7: useful part of patient prep and trust

column4: patient sees access status → understands who has access, for how long, and recent activity → keeps trust in the flow
column7: important trust layer, but access is already granted in this journey state

column4: patient receives doctor plan or request → opens plan from main screen → sees required analyses / next steps → chooses one item to complete
column7: core follow-up job after doctor review

column4: patient opens requested item → uploads corresponding analysis → result is attached to the relevant plan item → completion status updates
column7: closes the loop between doctor request and patient action

column4: patient receives reminder / request / plan notification → opens notification → lands on a clear action page → completes or reviews the needed step
column7: useful accelerator, can be simplified in first pilot

column4: patient opens Vasily from main screen → asks question by text or voice → receives trusted explanation / guidance → returns to preparation or learning path
column7: supports support/knowledge job, not core prep proof

column4: patient enters diabetes school / knowledge area → explores relevant topic or assigned material → improves understanding → returns to main flow if needed
column7: valuable between visits, but not core for first pilot

column4: patient completes prep or current doctor request → main screen shows ready / no urgent tasks → patient either waits for next doctor action or uses knowledge support
